extraction.one_line_summary: DEMAR Arnold won a chaotic sprint by bike throw after GAVIRIA and VAN POPPEL were taken out in final kilometer positioning battles
tactical_patterns: QUICKSTEP controlled tempo with inverted tactics—harder to keep gap at four minutes than close it down.
KÄMNA Leonard contested intermediate sprint at 28km for bonus seconds despite being 39s down on GC overall.
Final 5km headwind sprint meant late launchers like DEMAR coming from behind had advantage over early movers.

rider_signals.DEMAR Arnold: positive: back-to-back wins shows form peak, found clean air after chaos took out lead-out trains
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: neutral: launched early possibly due to VAN POPPEL crash disrupting QUICKSTEP train sequencing
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: taken out in final kilometer positioning, suggests positioning vulnerability in messy sprints
rider_signals.KÄMNA Leonard: positive: aggressive bonus second hunting at intermediate sprint 28km out while 39s down reveals GC ambition
